siteDescription: An editorial guide to the Mornington Peninsula, rebuilt as a sharper cover page with weekly curation, featured stories, venue picks, and a clear newsletter invitation.

hero.title: The smarter way to open a Mornington Peninsula weekend.
hero.dek: Less brochure, more editorial cover. Peninsula Insider should feel like the front page of a publication that already knows where you should book first, where you should linger, and what is actually worth the drive.
hero.meta: Edited by Peninsula Insider · Updated 9 April 2026
highlights: A lead story with a clear point of view
Fast paths to eat, stay, do, and plan
Handpicked venues instead of generic grids

sectionLinks.label: Eat & Drink
sectionLinks.note: Long lunches, cellar doors, and restaurants with actual editorial judgment.

sectionLinks.label: Stay
sectionLinks.note: Hotels, villas, and weekend bases chosen for mood, not inventory size.

sectionLinks.label: Do
sectionLinks.note: Walks, hot springs, beaches, and the things visitors ask about most.

sectionLinks.label: Plan
sectionLinks.note: Quick ways to turn a Saturday window into a coherent Peninsula day.

featuredStory.title: The autumn weekend locals keep to themselves
featuredStory.dek: A sharper front page starts with a proper lead, one opinionated dispatch that gives the whole site a centre of gravity. This week it is the argument for slower lunches, one coastal walk, and fewer forgettable stops.
bullets: Why April is the month when the Peninsula feels most earned
Three bookings worth making before Friday afternoon
One walk, one lunch, and one cellar door to anchor the weekend

weekendPlanner.title: Three moves, one clean itinerary
weekendPlanner.dek: The homepage should help a visitor act quickly. These cards preview the kind of concise editorial planning block the live product needs.

cards.title: A candlelit first night in Red Hill
cards.note: Lead with one serious booking, then stop. Save the rest of the weekend for daylight.

cards.title: Stretch lunch until the afternoon dissolves
cards.note: Choose one winery restaurant with a view, a second glass, and nowhere urgent to be.

cards.title: Cold air, a cliff walk, then coffee on the way out
cards.note: A lighter finish gives the whole weekend shape instead of ending in traffic and regret.

venueStrip.title: The kind of shortlist people actually save
venueStrip.dek: These are not database cards. They are editorial prompts for the next build phase, a tighter strip of flagship venues with enough context to earn the click.

venues.name: Tedesca Osteria
venues.blurb: The meal you plan around, not the fallback after a cellar door.

venues.name: Jackalope
venues.blurb: Still the Peninsula's most cinematic check-in when you want the weekend to feel decisive.

venues.name: Pt Leo Estate
venues.blurb: One of the easiest ways to combine sculpture, lunch, and a serious vineyard outlook.

venues.name: Cape Schanck
venues.blurb: When the weekend needs wind, cliffs, and a clean reset before Melbourne again.

journal.title: Recent stories with enough personality to matter

articles.title: Where to go for a long lunch on the Mornington Peninsula
articles.dek: The flagship intent piece. A ranked shortlist, not a sprawling maybe-list.

articles.title: Tedesca, Tractor, Laura: three Italian dinners and the argument between them
articles.dek: A stronger editorial engine means naming the differences, not pretending all prestige dinners are interchangeable.

articles.title: The quiet side of the Peninsula: a weekend in Flinders, not Red Hill
articles.dek: Proof that the site gets better when it helps people choose a mood, not just a suburb.

newsletter.title: The Insider's Letter, every Thursday.
newsletter.dek: One sharp note before the weekend: what to book, what to skip, what the Peninsula looks like right now.
benefits: Three edited recommendations, not twenty tabs to sort later
Early access to new guides, lists, and itineraries
The weekend's most useful booking and timing notes in five minutes
